title: Houses for Rent in North Collinwood, Cleveland
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in North Collinwood, Cleveland OH
metaDescription: Houses for rent in North Collinwood (44110), Cleveland's lakefront East Side neighborhood. Rent ranges, transit, and Section 8-friendly homes. Book a showing.
primaryKeyword: houses for rent North Collinwood cleveland
answerBox: North Collinwood is Cleveland's lakefront East Side neighborhood, largely in ZIP code 44110, home to the Waterloo Arts District. Two- and three-bedroom rental homes here typically run from about $750 to $1,200 a month, and Rent Finder Cleveland manages Section 8-friendly homes throughout the area.

sections.h2: Renting a house in North Collinwood
body: North Collinwood is Cleveland's lakefront neighborhood on the far northeast side, largely within **ZIP code 44110**, running along Lake Erie north of St. Clair Avenue. It's best known for the **Waterloo Arts District**, a stretch of Waterloo Road lined with galleries, music venues, and studios. Housing stock is mostly early-1900s bungalows, Cape Cods, and two-family duplexes on modest lots.

Many of the residential blocks closest to the lake feature small single-family homes originally built for workers at the rail yards and lakefront industry that shaped this part of the city. Farther inland toward Waterloo Road, the mix shifts toward two-family duplexes, several of which sit within walking distance of the arts district's galleries and coffee shops.

Rent Finder Cleveland manages Section 8-friendly rental homes throughout the Collinwood area, including North Collinwood. [Book a free showing](/p/book-showing) to see current availability, or explore [houses for rent across Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) if you're comparing neighborhoods.

Renters who want easy lake access, or who work at nearby employers along the St. Clair Avenue and I-90 corridor, often prioritize North Collinwood specifically over other East Side options for its compact stretch of galleries along Waterloo Road and its shoreline parks.

sections.h2: How much does it cost to rent in North Collinwood?
body: Two-bedroom homes in North Collinwood typically rent from about $750 to $1,000 a month, and three-bedroom homes run roughly $950 to $1,200. These ranges sit within the broader $700–$1,800 span across the East Side homes Rent Finder Cleveland manages, where the typical rent is close to $1,000. Citywide, Cleveland's median asking rent was $1,250 a month as of July 2026, per Zumper, with three-bedroom units averaging $1,350 — North Collinwood's rental stock generally prices below both figures.

rows: Larger duplex or converted multi-family home

body: North Collinwood's lakefront location and arts district give it a distinct character within Cleveland's East Side.

items: **ZIP code:** 44110, on Cleveland's far northeast lakefront
**Transit:** GCRTA bus routes along Waterloo Road and St. Clair Avenue; I-90 runs along the neighborhood's southern edge for quick access downtown or toward the Lake County suburbs
**Arts & culture:** The Waterloo Arts District, with monthly gallery walks along Waterloo Road
**Geography:** Directly on Lake Erie, making it one of the city's northernmost residential neighborhoods
**Nearby suburbs:** Borders Euclid to the east, a short drive along St. Clair Avenue or I-90

sections.h2: Section 8 and vouchers in North Collinwood
body: Every home we manage in North Collinwood accepts Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and is HUD-inspection-ready. CMHA, the Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority, runs [voucher applications year-round](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php) with no fixed closing date. As with the rest of Cleveland, there's no city ordinance protecting voucher holders from source-of-income discrimination, so ask directly whether a specific landlord accepts vouchers. Read our full [Section 8 housing guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for details on applying and payment standards.

sections.h2: Touring and applying for a home in North Collinwood
body: If you find a North Collinwood listing you like, book a free showing to walk through the home in person — there's no cost or obligation. When you're ready to move forward, you can complete your rental application online through our secure DoorLoop portal. Feel free to ask about voucher eligibility for a specific property before you schedule a tour.

Renters drawn to North Collinwood for the Waterloo Arts District and lakefront setting often also look at homes farther along St. Clair Avenue toward Euclid, so it's worth comparing a few nearby listings before deciding.

North Collinwood sits directly on Lake Erie, while [South Collinwood](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-south-collinwood-cleveland/) is the inland portion of the same broader Collinwood district, centered on the historic Nottingham area farther from the shoreline. Both fall largely within ZIP 44110, and Rent Finder Cleveland manages voucher-friendly homes in both.

faq.q: What ZIP code is North Collinwood in?
faq.a: North Collinwood is in ZIP code 44110, on Cleveland's far northeast lakefront along Lake Erie. It includes the Waterloo Arts District along Waterloo Road.

faq.q: Are there Section 8 houses for rent in North Collinwood?
faq.a: Yes. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland manages in North Collinwood accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Book a free showing to see current availability.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent a house in North Collinwood?
faq.a: Two-bedroom homes typically run $750 to $1,000 a month, and three-bedroom homes run roughly $950 to $1,200, in line with rents across Cleveland's East Side.

faq.q: What's the difference between North and South Collinwood?
faq.a: North Collinwood sits directly on Lake Erie and includes the Waterloo Arts District, while South Collinwood is the inland part of the same district, centered on the historic Nottingham area. Both are largely in ZIP 44110.

faq.q: How do I get to downtown Cleveland from North Collinwood?
faq.a: I-90 runs along the neighborhood's southern edge, offering a direct drive downtown, and several GCRTA bus routes connect Waterloo Road and St. Clair Avenue to the rest of the city.

faq.q: How do I apply for a house in North Collinwood?
faq.a: Book a free showing to see the home first, then submit your rental application online through our secure DoorLoop portal once you're ready to move forward. You can ask about voucher eligibility for a specific property before scheduling a tour.
